    Rio erupts are Martinelli wins it for Brazil in the 95th minute

Brazil's Martinelli scores late winner to knock Japan out of World Cup

Gabriel Martinelli scored late injury time to give Brazil a 2-1 win over Japan in the round of 32 at the World Cup on Monday.
Jun 29, 2026, 07:35 pm - PA

Match Timeline & Momentum
Momentum
This graph shows how much pressure a team applied during the match. Taller columns indicate stronger attacking periods, based on things like possession, shots, goals, big chances, and other key stats.

BRA
JPN
KOHTFT

Commentary

Brazil come from a goal down at half-time to beat Japan 2-1 in a thrilling World Cup last-32 tie. Sano profited from a sloppy pass, and some poor Casemiro defending, to fire Japan ahead just before the half-hour mark in Houston. Brazil offered very little in the first half but were a lot better in the second, which told when Casemiro - having earlier had an easy header saved off the line - powered his next header in at the back post. Vinicius Jr was denied a wonder goal by a fine Suzuki save against the post, but substitute Martinelli curled in a fine winner in the 96th minute after being played in by Guimaraes to snatch the win before extra time. Brazil ended the match with an xG of 1.72 to Japan's 0.23, with the Asian side made to pay for sitting back a little too much in the end. Ancelotti's side will now take on Ivory Coast or Norway in the last 16. Thanks for joining!

90'+11'

FULL-TIME: BRAZIL 2-1 JAPAN

90'+9'

We are into the ninth minute of what was supposed to be six added on. We had the stoppage for Casemiro's injury, plus the goal, so there may be another minute to go.

90'+8'
Substitution

Guimaraes, fresh on the back of that assist, makes way for Danilo.

Danilo SantosD. SantosMidfielder
Bruno GuimarãesB. GuimarãesMidfielder
90'+7'
Substitution

Japan also male a change as Ogawa comes on for Maeda. It's surely too late now for a response?

Koki OgawaK. OgawaForward
Daizen MaedaD. MaedaForward
90'+5'

Guimaraes found himself in the pocket and did well to slide the ball into Martinelli. This was all about the finish from the Brazil forward, though.
